name: SpectrumAnalyzer
author: Nguyen
description: Runs spectrum analysis on the whole library, album by album: detects fake lossless / lossy transcodes / fake hi-res (upsampled) / fake 24-bit (upscaled) via frequency-cutoff, noise-shelf, aliasing-image and bit-depth heuristics, and stores a spectrogram (base64 PNG) per song. Parallel per-album scanning, per-track deep (whole-file) dark-master analysis, manual verification flags. Tracks are analyzed once and re-analyzed only when the underlying file changes; results are removed automatically by the core cleanup task.
_comment: Pre-0.1.0 development history (0.1.0..0.6.2 before the version reset) is preserved in CHANGELOG.md.

versions.changelog: AudioMuse-AI 3.0 support (multi-server canonical fp ids). Scans, re-analysis, deep scans and the analysis hook now translate native media-server ids to the canonical ids score is keyed by (track_server_map), fixing 'everything not_in_score' on 3.x. Existing data migrates automatically and losslessly: the results FK becomes ON UPDATE CASCADE so the core's id relabel carries our rows (verdicts, spectrograms, verified flags, MD5 cache all preserved) — this also unblocks core 3.0 upgrades that were failing at boot due to the old FK. If you are still on 2.x: update the plugin BEFORE upgrading the core. If your 3.0 upgrade is stuck: update the plugin + Apply restart, the next boot completes it. Multi-server: manual scans cover every configured server, re-analysis downloads from the server that supplied the track, cron scans follow the schedule's server scope. Task rows are keyed by the RQ job id so the 3.x janitor no longer fails a running scan as orphaned, and hook-analyzed tracks now store the scan fingerprint so the next changed scan skips them without re-downloading. Works on both 2.6.2 and 3.0.1+.

Overview: LOWPASSED counts are now visible — a total in the header stats and a per-album 'Lowpassed' column (amber), both excluding manually verified tracks. These are the ambiguous possibly-dark-master files worth a deep scan. UI: secondary buttons (Filter, Re-analyze, Deep analyze, Deep scan all non-CLEAN) restyled amber/brown — the core's default gray-on-white was barely visible; a queued Deep analyze button now looks properly disabled.

Search matches album, artist and song title (shows albums containing a match). Deep-scan queueing is idempotent: repeat clicks while the 'deep scan queued' tag is set enqueue nothing, and the button is disabled meanwhile (a plain Re-analyze clears a stuck tag). New overview button: 'Deep scan all non-CLEAN' queues a whole-file deep scan for every unverified non-CLEAN track in the library and reports how many were queued.

First release. Parallel album-by-album scanning (orchestrator + per-album worker tasks), change detection (metadata fingerprint + audio MD5), verdicts CLEAN / CONSISTENT_LOSSY / LOWPASSED / UPSAMPLED / UPSCALED / TRANSCODED_LOSSY / FAKE_SUSPECT calibrated against ground-truth fixtures, aliasing-image and bit-depth probes, per-track deep whole-file dark-master analysis with status tags, manual verification flags, per-status album filters, base64 spectrograms, on_song_analyzed hook, optional weekly incremental cron.
